meta.title: AI Loop claim registry
meta.purpose: Structured source-of-truth for non-deal figures used by the AI Loop website: tape, debate, materials, concentration, look-through and correction watchlist.

statusKey.verified: Source directly supports the displayed claim.
statusKey.reported: Credible reporting supports the claim, but it is not a company filing or primary announcement.
statusKey.estimate: Analyst/model/forecast estimate; not a hard fact.
statusKey.contested: Claim is part of a live debate or has methodology caveats.
statusKey.uncertain: Weak source, vendor-only source, or source does not fully support the exact number.
statusKey.needs_update: Current site wording should be changed before being treated as verified.

sources.title: Banks get creative and look further afield as AI-fueled debt soars

claims.label: 2026 CAPEX
claims.displayText: ~$725B planned hyperscaler capex in 2026
claims.notes: Use ~$725B as the sourced anchor. If retaining ~$725-800B, add a second source for the upper bound.

claims.label: OPENAI
claims.displayText: OpenAI ~$852B valuation; IPO slips toward 2027
claims.notes: Needs a directly linked source in this registry before being used as a headline tape claim.

claims.label: ANTHROPIC
claims.displayText: Anthropic ~$965B valuation; run-rate ~$47B
claims.notes: Run-rate is annualized, not realized revenue. Keep the methodology caveat.

claims.label: SPACEX
claims.displayText: SpaceX ~$1.75T IPO valuation; targeting/raised ~$75B
claims.notes: Reuters source supports planned IPO price/raise/valuation before listing. If page says 'raised', cite post-listing source too.

claims.label: STARGATE
claims.displayText: Stargate >$400B committed/planned over three years
claims.notes: Prefer wording 'planned / announced / committed' rather than cash already spent.

claims.label: GE VERNOVA
claims.displayText: $163B backlog
claims.notes: Primary company results source.

claims.label: E-WASTE
claims.displayText: 22.3% of e-waste properly recycled
claims.notes: Global E-waste Monitor figure.

claims.label: OPENAI CHIP
claims.displayText: Jalapeno custom inference chip with Broadcom
claims.notes: Reuters supports custom chip collaboration and inference focus.

claims.label: AMD
claims.displayText: AMD warrant up to roughly 10% to OpenAI, tied to 6GW deployment milestones
claims.notes: Use 'up to roughly 10%' only as an interpretation of warrant share count versus diluted shares.

claims.label: MSFT
claims.displayText: Microsoft owns roughly 27% of OpenAI; OpenAI has $250B Azure services commitment
claims.notes: Primary Microsoft source supports both figures.

claims.label: NVIDIA to OPENAI
claims.displayText: Split the old up-to-$100B Nvidia/OpenAI LOI from the newer reported ~$30B investment

recommendedModel: original up-to-$100B systems/investment partnership = LOI or superseded
~$30B Nvidia investment in OpenAI round = reported until final close confirmed

claims.notes: This is the most important model/graph ambiguity.

claims.label: AMAZON to ANTHROPIC
claims.displayText: Replace '$8B+' with staged investment wording

normalizedValue.recommendedValue: $8B prior + $5B new + up to $20B more; Anthropic commits >$100B to AWS over 10 years

claims.notes: The old '$8B+' is technically true but understates the current arrangement.

claims.label: OPENAI to BROADCOM
claims.displayText: 10GW custom accelerator deployment by 2029
claims.notes: Use Reuters for Jalapeno and a separate source that explicitly supports 10GW/2029.

claims.label: Hardware reuse
claims.displayText: 90.9% Microsoft server reuse and recycle rate, FY24
claims.notes: Primary Microsoft sustainability source.

claims.label: Hardware reuse
claims.displayText: Oracle reports 99.6% reuse
claims.notes: Primary Oracle report.

claims.label: Hardware reuse
claims.displayText: Refurbishing can save up to 85% of manufacturing energy
claims.notes: Keep '?' marker or remove until independently sourced.

claims.label: Hardware reuse
claims.displayText: Only ~28% of operators track decommissioned gear
claims.notes: Keep '?' marker or remove until independently sourced.

claims.label: Power and grid
claims.displayText: GE Vernova power-equipment backlog is $163B
claims.notes: Same source as tape.

claims.label: Power and grid
claims.displayText: Microsoft-backed restart of Three Mile Island Unit 1 via Constellation agreement
claims.notes: Specialist outlet; replace with Constellation/Microsoft primary if added later.

claims.label: Power and grid
claims.displayText: Meta lined up roughly 5-6GW of nuclear reactors/projects
claims.notes: Use as planned/project pipeline, not delivered power.

claims.label: Water
claims.displayText: A big data center can draw up to 5M gallons per day
claims.notes: Good as contextual upper-bound, not typical usage.

claims.label: Water
claims.displayText: Correct Texas water figure

normalizedValue.recommendedValue: roughly 25B gallons/year today and 29B-161B gallons/year by 2030, depending on buildout/cooling assumptions

claims.notes: Earlier 399B number appears inconsistent with HARC-linked reporting.

claims.label: Water
claims.displayText: U.S. residential power prices rose 7.1% in 2025, double inflation
claims.notes: Keep exact year/period in visible copy.

claims.label: Mag 7 concentration
claims.displayText: Seven companies make up about one-third of the S&P 500
claims.notes: Replace or augment with S&P Dow Jones, BoE, Goldman, Apollo or JPM source when available.

claims.label: ETF concentration
claims.displayText: ~33% of SMH is Nvidia + TSMC + Broadcom
claims.notes: Needs direct ETF holdings scrape/source; keep '?' marker.

claims.label: Enterprise AI ROI
claims.displayText: MIT Project NANDA found about 95% of enterprise AI pilots show no measurable P&L impact
claims.notes: Use as study finding, not universal law.

claims.label: Hyperscaler capex pressure
claims.displayText: Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta projected to spend over $600B in 2026 on AI-related infrastructure
claims.notes: This supports a lower bound separate from the broader $725B hyperscaler capex tape.
